mechanism_narrative: Sustained momentum in real retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and leads to positive forward equity returns as earnings expectations are revised upwards.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in RSAFS has a rank information coefficient below 0.03 with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a key threshold, it signals a very tight labor market. This leads to wage-push inflation, compressing corporate profit margins and causing negative equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE is below 4.5% and falling.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating inflation and an accommodative central bank (low Fed Funds rate) creates deeply negative real interest rates. This condition forces investors into riskier assets like equities to seek real returns, boosting valuations.
candidate_trigger: YoY change in CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 2.0%
failure_condition: A portfolio strategy based on this signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold S&P 500 strategy on a risk-adjusted basis over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, positive momentum in industrial production indicates that the supply side of the economy is keeping pace with strong demand, alleviating bottleneck fears and supporting corporate profitability, thus driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO 3-month momentum and S&P 500 20-day returns is negative during periods identified as late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate over a 6-month period signals an aggressive monetary tightening cycle. This increases the discount rate applied to corporate earnings and restricts credit, leading to lower equity valuations.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return is not statistically significantly lower (p > 0.05) in periods following a trigger compared to non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls is a high-frequency, high-confidence signal of economic strength. This positive surprise factor drives immediate positive sentiment and short-term equity market gains.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a PAYEMS release that beats consensus expectations by one standard deviation is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and retail sales indicates a broad-based contraction in both investment and consumption demand. This interaction signals a high probability of an economic slowdown, causing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in HOUST < 0 and 3-month change in RSAFS < 0
failure_condition: The hit rate (percentage of correct directional predictions) of this combined signal is less than 55% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth is the most fundamental indicator of aggregate economic health. This strength translates directly into higher aggregate corporate earnings, driving equity markets higher over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The quarter-over-quarter growth rate of GDPC1 has a beta coefficient that is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a regression predicting next-quarter S&P 500 returns.
